# Transcrição: 12-ACTIVITY- What to Share

- **Fonte:** 12-ACTIVITY- What to Share
- **Duração:** 03:06
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:03]

Okay, now that you understand the importance of finding things in your life that are worth sharing with others, those subtle things that will connect you with other people, I want you to make a list. We have a worksheet for you to actually put on this list the things that you think will help you connect with other people. The things like for me include elementary school teacher and parent of two children, parent of a son, parent of a daughter, husband to a wife, um, golf lover, cat owner, all of these things that when I mention it to people, if they have the same thing in common, there's an instant connection. I want you to make this list as well. I want you to make this list over the course of time. I think one of the mistakes people make is they always assume that their first idea is their best idea and it almost never is. I want you to keep this list under your desk for a while and I want you to be looking at it often. I want you to be thinking about what else about me makes me interesting, unique, or maybe just an easy way to connect to other people. What about me do other people also possess? Because that's what we're really looking for. We're looking for an aspect of you that will also connect with an aspect of other people. When those two things come together, people trust you, they believe in you, they want to hear you. The other thing I'd like you to do is to find people in your life who know you well and ask them what might go on the list. What things about you make them feel closer to you? A lot of times we don't know what people see in us that makes them believe in us. You're going to have to ask that awkward question. You're going to have to ask your spouse or your partner, your children, your friends, your colleagues, even the people who work with you. What about me makes me connected to you? What makes you feel like you and I are on the same team, that we have the same beliefs, that we have the same things in common, that we move through this world in the same way? You need to ask as many people who know you well this question, the longer your list of things that you can subtly include in conversation to make people more connected to you, make people feel more connected with you, the better off you're going to be in business and in life. When people believe in you, when they trust you, when they want to listen to you, when they see you as someone worthy of their attention and their time, you're going to do a lot better. You're going to sell more products, you're going to find and retain more clients, you're going to achieve the goals that you're trying to achieve. This is an easy and highly effective way to do it and the first step is to make that list. Commit it to memory. Start practicing, inserting some of these things into your daily conversation, but first start

## [03:03 - 03:06]

with the list and ask others to help you. Good luck.
